EliMZ_Book.log:
• Added a direct argument on the forceFinish methods of the Anime classes. This will make it possible to finish an anime directly(applying the target value), according to the direction.
  • Fixed an issue that the AnimeCollection class was calling a wrong function name to force finish all anime childrens.
  • Fixed an issue that plugins on subfolders inside the plugins folders are not being scanned for updated versions.
  • Made some changes on the style of the version update manager.
  • Add a way to show an update log of the plugin you click to update.

EliMZ_CameraManager.log:
1.3.0

  • Added a new argument called “Focus Character” to the plugin commands Scroll to Character. Before, the camera was always focusing on the character after reaching it. Now, you can decide if you want the camera to just reach him and not focus. Meaning, the camera will not keep following him or be attached to it.
  • Added a new feature called camera offset. Let you add offset values, in tiles, for the camera X and Y axis. Can set an initial value with plugin parameters and change it mid game with plugin commands.
  • Added a new plugin command that lets you remove the camera focus to any character.

1.2.0

  • Added a new plugin parameter that lets you decide if you want for PIXI to round pixels on rendering or not. By default it is set to true. Before the plugin was setting that hardcoded to true. Now you have the freedom to change that if you do not want. When true, the movement is less smooth.
  • Added a new argument on the Scroll To Character plugin command, that lets the camera follow a character that is moving until it reaches him, then the camera starts moving with him.
  • Fixed an issue that when activating manual control, sometimes you could get a delay to move the camera.
  • Fixed an issue that the plugin was rounding the tilemap origin coordinates even when using smooth scroll.
  • Fixed an issue that the smooth scroll was using a wrong calculation that was making the scroll not so smooth on the first frames of scrolling.
  • Fixed an issue that maps with horizontal or vertical loops are conflicting with the lock/unlock scroll limit feature.
  • Changed the description of the arguments from the plugin command “Screen Scroll Limit”. It now explains better what each argument will do.
  • Changed the description of the argument “Change Control” from the plugin command “Manual Control”. It had another description that was not related to it.
  • Improved help file.

EliMZ_ChoiceManager.log: • Fixed the crash caused by the last update: “TypeError Cannot set property 'Game_Picture_initialize' of undefined”

EliMZ_ControlEvents.log:
• Removed the dependency of Eli Camera Manager. Now this plugin can work with or without Camera Manager.
  • This will break the previous version. So continuing a previous save file with a previous version of this plugin will not work. You need to restart a new game
  • Added a Focus Camera option to the Control Character plugin command.
  • Added camera movement modes for controlling a character and returning control to the player.
  • Added compatibility with Eli Jump System so controlled characters can jump using player rules.
  • Changed Help file

EliMZ_DiagonalCharacters.log:
• Fixed an issue that the Enable/Disable Diagonal Move plugin command effect was being removed when a character changed image file. Meaning, if you disable a character from moving diagonally, if he changed image and that image has a diagonal movement/sprite tag, the plugin command was being overridden by it.
  • Added a new argument called “Default” on the Enable/Disable Diagonal Move plugin command. This argument will remove the forced state of the plugin command and let the characters get their diagonal movement from their filenames again.
  • Fixed an issue that characters with empty filenames are always able to walk diagonally
  • Fixed character diagonal speed being applied when a character tries to move diagonally and fails.
  • Some minor performance improvements
  • All plugin code is documented with JSDOcs.

EliMZ_ExpByLevel.log:
• Fixed the plugin parameter ‘Rule’ being with a wrong default parameter
  • Fixed an issue that the plugin was calculating the total exp by the enemy troops, by iterating through them all, without considering if they are dead. I believe that could cause some problems with hidden enemies or enemies that escape from battle. Don’t know for sure, but it is fixed just in case.
  • Some minor performance improvement and code clean up
  • Fixed the plugin not using the default database messages to show how many exp the actor gains after the battle
  • Fixed plugin not using ‘actor.finalExpRate()’, which considers the gain of exp for members inside and outside the battle, according to the database option set
  • Some code clean up.
  • Plugin is now properly documented with JSDOCs!

EliMZ_FrameSkip.log: • Added a new plugin parameter: “Ignore Game Timer”. When set to true, the Game Timer count will not be affected by the fast forward.

EliMZ_GlobalText.log:
• A big performance improvement using automatic mode: Before, all texts are drawn with the drawTextEx method when automatic mode is true. Even when they do not have any escape code. Now the plugin checks if there is an escape code first, before using drawTextEx.
  • Added a new plugin parameter that lets you set up what windows you want to exclude from the plugin. So the plugin will not apply global text to them in any circumstance. Before, this was hardcoded for this windows: Window_Shop, Window_ItemList, Window_NumberInput, Window_SkillList
  • Changed help file

EliMZ_PressStart.log:
• Added two new properties of the Press Start Sprite to animate:
    • Angle & Hue.
  • Added Incremental type of animation. Unlike Initial/Target, this kind of animation will just add or subtract a value from the property, on each frame, as long as the animation lasts.

EliMZ_SaveFileManager.log:
• ATTENTION! This version breaks the previous one. If you update, you will need to start a new game and set up the plugin parameters again.
  • Added a RogueLike / PermaDeath system:
    • Can prevent the player from saving into another save slot instead of the current one
    • Can delete save files automatically before the game goes into the Game Over scene
    • Added a switch that reflects the value of the permadeath.
    • Added an optional window after hitting the new game to choose permadeath or not.
  • Optionally added a confirmation window when press ok on the slot files of the save and load scenes.
  • Optionally add Copy and Delete commands to the confirmation window, so you can copy and delete slot files from the load and save scene!
  • Fixed a rare issue that could happen when deleting a save file.
  • Several performance upgrades.

EliMZ_TpSystem.log:
• Added two new plugin parameters that let you optionally assign default formulas (Charge Tp and Action Tp) for all actors and enemies that miss their respective formula note tags.
  • Hopefully fixed the Skill Do Not Consume TP not properly working.
  • Removed some useless console log around the code

EliMZ_TestUpdateLog1.log:
• Added some utility functions to check for event triggers.
  • Added a plugin parameter to control how the Iterate List note tag works. Check help file here.
  • Added BattleManager.beforeSetup and afterSetup. To be used by other plugins.
  • Added a new class to run a game interpreter inside code(passive Game_Interpreter).
  • Added a way to verify how a battle was triggered: Map Random Encounters, Event Command, and Event Command(Same as random Encounter).
  • Fixed an issue that the Eli.Input.mapGamepadButton was checking for defaultKeyboard keys when needed to check default gamepad keys.

EliMZ_NewPluginTest.log:
• Added some utility functions to check for event triggers.
  • Added a plugin parameter to control how the Iterate List note tag works. Check help file here.
  • Added BattleManager.beforeSetup and afterSetup. To be used by other plugins.
  • Added a new class to run a game interpreter inside code(passive Game_Interpreter).
  • Added a way to verify how a battle was triggered: Map Random Encounters, Event Command, and Event Command(Same as random Encounter).
  • Fixed an issue that the Eli.Input.mapGamepadButton was checking for defaultKeyboard keys when needed to check default gamepad keys.
